subject: [N5] 36 action items from 📭 2025-10-14 external-michael-maher
body:

I extracted 36 action items from your 📭 2025-10-14 external-michael-maher meeting (20251028-132902_Meetings).
Please review and reply with your approval:

   📁 Operations | 🏷 meeting

**4. **

**5. ### Key Decisions and Agreements**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**6. **Careerspan will be included in Cornell Johnson CMC tool evaluation** - Michael confirmed he wants Careerspan "in the mix" when CMC evaluates replacements for Vmock in the coming weeks. This matters because it creates formal evaluation pathway after months of informal advocacy. Michael explicitly stated "I think yours is better, but I have to make sure they have to think that too."**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**7. **Evaluation timeline: 2-3 weeks out** - CMC won't start concentrated testing until "when the crunch is over" (likely after recruiting season peak). This matters because it sets realistic expectation that decision won't happen immediately, despite urgency around Vmock costs.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**8. **Strategy shift: Prove tool quality first, negotiate pricing later** - Michael advised "I don't think the money is the issue at the moment. I think that we have to persuade that your tool is something that will work." This matters because Vrijen was leading with "free" messaging, but Michael is signaling that team credibility and tool effectiveness are the real barriers, not price.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**9. **No direct outreach to David Capaldi yet** - Michael advised waiting on direct David outreach until after CMC team evaluation process begins. This matters because it provides clear sequencing: let Michael advocate internally first, then engage directly if needed.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**10. ### Strategic Context**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**11. Cornell's Positioning:** The school is under "enormous budget pressure" with ongoing cost-cutting initiatives across departments. CMC is specifically looking to replace Vmock ($20K/year, "shitty" product per Michael) but is distracted by broader organizational efficiency pressures. The challenge isn't financial - it's organizational inertia and the fact that "schools use the ones they've always used or what the other schools use all the time."**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Product | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**12. Careerspan's Positioning:** Vrijen revealed major strategic pivot underway - moving away from direct B2B sales ("not having fun selling") toward building personal brands and using that to fund free product distribution. This changes the value prop: instead of selling career tools, they're offering institutional support as alumni founders while monetizing through personal brand/expertise. Cornell represents both validation opportunity and personal mission (give back to alma mater).**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Product | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**13. The Advocacy Dynamic:** Michael is clearly supportive ("I think yours is better than anything else I've seen so far") but cautious about overpromising. He's positioning as internal champion but needs team buy-in. His credibility depends on tool quality, not just personal relationship. Key tension: Michael recognizes Cornell doesn't adequately support alumni founders, creating both guilt and opportunity.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**14. Competitive Landscape:** Vmock is incumbent at $20K, Quincy mentioned as alternative at "less than half" cost, Big Resume and Exponent mentioned as other tools in ecosystem. Michael characterizes most tools as "suck" and "shitty" but schools default to familiar names. Careerspan needs to overcome "not the current vendor" disadvantage through product superiority demonstration.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Product | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**15. ### Critical Next Action**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**16. Owner:** Michael Maher**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**18. Timeline:** Next 1-2 weeks (before evaluation committee convenes in "a few weeks")**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**19. Purpose:** Build personal conviction and identify specific selling points to advocate to CMC team for Careerspan's inclusion in formal evaluation process**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**20. No deliverables promised by Michael - his commitments are actions (test tool, advocate internally), not content**

   📁 Operations | 🏷 deliverable
   📝 Deliverable from meeting...
